title: Inter-island transfers can eat your entire day
note: A 'short hop' between islands often means: jeepney to port (1–2h), wait for ferry (1–3h), crossing (2–5h), then tricycle from the port. Plan a full buffer day on both ends — not just one.

title: Island internet flips between fast and near-zero, often within an hour
note: Mobile data on PLDT or Smart can drop from solid LTE to nothing on smaller islands without warning. Download maps, work files, and anything you need before leaving the city.

title: Ferry schedules are optimistic — weather cancels with little notice
note: Fast ferries get pulled in rough seas sometimes only hours before departure. Roro ferries are slower but more resilient to cancellation. Never book a flight out the same day as a sea crossing.

title: ATMs run out on popular islands over weekends
note: Even in tourist areas like El Nido or Coron, ATMs run dry on Fridays and public holidays. Carry enough pesos for 2–3 extra days before heading to a smaller island.

title: Resort island prices have risen sharply since 2019
note: El Nido and Coron have roughly doubled in price. Budget travel is still possible but requires booking ahead and eating where locals eat — tourist menus are now closer to European pricing.
